name: GOS
summary: Governed Operational Spec authoring, TypeScript tooling, lifecycle policy, Swift runtime consumption, and app-safe mediation under Core law.
invariants: GOS is subordinate to Core law and provides operational mediation only.
GOS has a bipartite shape: TypeScript tooling plus Swift runtime consumption.
Runtime consumers should use resolved/app-safe GOS views, not raw compiled spec JSON.
Bundle activation requires review, activation policy, provenance, and lifecycle audit.
allowedWork: Maintain GOS authoring, compiler, bundle, lifecycle, and review records.
Maintain Swift GOS runtime consumption boundaries in Core/AACI where present.
Preserve app-safe GOS summaries for Scribe/Veridia/CloudClinic.
Update GOS schemas, TS tooling, Swift contracts, and docs together when contracts change.
forbiddenWork: Expose raw compiled spec JSON as app law.
Use GOS as clinical, regulatory, or finalizing authority.
knownGaps: Production GOS governance and operator lifecycle hardening remain future work.
GOS app consumption must remain minimal and honest until app surfaces mature.
notes: Use HealthOS Forge MCP only for future construction tooling; it is distinct from GOS runtime behavior.
Territory records do not activate bundles or authorize GOS use.
